commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for real Nostr relay proof, conflict/current-main adaptation, and the media URL disclosure finding; automation should not repair this PR without real setup proof.; Needs attention: the proposed media fallback can disclose raw local or internal media references in Nostr DMs.; Review finding: [P1] Drop the Nostr media fallback — extensions/nostr/src/channel.ts:32 (sha=6836683b2158cff57f2843d7be2ec23ffa0a9fd7)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Nostr DM subscription filtering, makes the Nostr gateway account task wait for abort, adds a sendMedia text fallback, and adds focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Configure the Nostr plugin and start the gateway: current main still passes a Filter[] into nostr-tools 2.23.3's single-Filter API and the Nostr start task resolves into the generic restart path; no live relay was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for real Nostr relay proof, conflict/current-main adaptation, and the media URL disclosure finding; automation should not repair this PR without real setup proof.

target.title: Nostr: restore DM delivery path
target.body:
## Summary
- pass a single DM subscription filter object to `subscribeMany()`
- keep `gateway.startAccount()` alive until abort instead of resolving immediately
- add a `sendMedia` fallback so outbound delivery can send attachment links over DM

## Root cause
`main` still had all three failures described in #32252:
1. `nostr-bus.ts` wrapped the DM filter in an array before calling `subscribeMany()`.
2. `channel.ts` resolved `startAccount()` immediately after startup instead of waiting for abort.
3. the outbound adapter only exposed `sendText`, so the generic delivery layer treated Nostr outbound as unconfigured.

## Testing
- `pnpm test extensions/nostr/src/channel.outbound.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/nostr/src/nostr-bus.subscribe.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/nostr/src/channel.test.ts`

Closes #32252
